description:
You are a build-/release-engineer in charge of preparing a quick “size & checksum”
    performance benchmark of today’s build artifacts.

    PRE-EXISTING MATERIAL  
    1. A directory “/home/user/build/bin/” already contains three freshly built
       ELF binaries:
            • /home/user/build/bin/tool_alpha
            • /home/user/build/bin/tool_beta
            • /home/user/build/bin/tool_gamma  
       (Their exact byte contents have been fixed by the CI system; do not modify
       them.)

    WHAT YOU MUST DO  
    1. Create a new directory “/home/user/benchmarks”.
    2. For each of the three binaries, collect the following metrics:
       a) Exact file size in bytes   → stat -c%s <file> (or equivalent).
       b) MD5 checksum               → md5sum <file>  (record only the hash, not the
                                              trailing filename nor extra spaces).
       c) SHA-256 checksum           → sha256sum <file> (again: hash only).
    3. Assemble a single TAB-separated text file named
       “/home/user/benchmarks/performance.tsv” with the **precise** layout below:

       Line 1 (header) must literally be:
           File<TAB>SizeBytes<TAB>MD5<TAB>SHA256

       Line ordering: alphabetical by basename
           tool_alpha
           tool_beta
           tool_gamma

       Example of the required shape (hash values shown here are dummies!):

           File    SizeBytes   MD5                             SHA256
           tool_alpha  12345  1d0e3a…       33ab54…
           tool_beta   67890  0bc9ff…       998877…
           tool_gamma  11111  88f0be…       aabbcc…

       IMPORTANT FORMAT RULES  
       • Fields separated by ONE ASCII <TAB> (0x09) – no extra spaces.
       • The file **must end with a single trailing newline**.
    4. Produce a short human-readable log file
       “/home/user/benchmarks/size_summary.log” with exactly two lines:

           TotalFiles: 3
           TotalBytes: <SUM>

       where <SUM> is the sum of the three individual byte-sizes you measured.
       No extra whitespace; newline at the end of the second line is mandatory.

    VERIFICATION POINTS (WHAT THE AUTOMATED TEST WILL CHECK)  
    • Directory “/home/user/benchmarks” exists.  
    • File “performance.tsv” exists, has four lines, is TAB-separated, header
      matches perfectly, lines are alphabetically ordered, size figures are
      correct, and MD5 / SHA-256 hashes match the real binaries bit-for-bit.  
    • File “size_summary.log” exists with the two exact lines and the correct
      total byte count.  
    • None of the original files under /home/user/build/bin/ are modified.

    DO NOT print the entire contents of the binaries to the terminal; they are
    treated as opaque artifacts.  Console output showing your intermediate commands
    and results is fine, but the two benchmark files above are the deliverables
    that determine success.
truth:
<!--
       These files already exist before the task starts.
       Automated tests will re-read them to calculate the expected metrics,
       so their contents are spelled out here verbatim.
    -->
    <file path="/home/user/build/bin/tool_alpha" perms="0644">
alpha build artifact v1.0
</file>

    <file path="/home/user/build/bin/tool_beta"  perms="0644">
beta build artifact v1.0
</file>

    <file path="/home/user/build/bin/tool_gamma" perms="0644">
gamma build artifact v1.0
</file>

    <!--
        Each file has a trailing newline (0x0A) and **nothing else**.
        Sizes therefore are:
            tool_alpha 26 bytes
            tool_beta  25 bytes
            tool_gamma 26 bytes
        TotalBytes = 77
        The automated grader will compute MD5/SHA-256 checksums on the fly and
        compare them with the values written by the agent into performance.tsv.
    -->

    <!-- Names and exact relative/absolute paths of files the agent must create -->
    <expected_create path="/home/user/benchmarks" type="dir" perms="0755" />
    <expected_create path="/home/user/benchmarks/performance.tsv" type="file" perms="0644" />
    <expected_create path="/home/user/benchmarks/size_summary.log" type="file" perms="0644" />
